title: Symmetrized convention for Tr_sigma(M^p B) used before it is defined; shared-spectrum identity implicit

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Where.** Statement of Lemma (Barrier update) [lem:bu], eq. (U), and eq. (total) in Step 4.

**The gap (consistency / faithfulness).** The quantities $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{-2}B)$ and
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{-1}B)$ appear in the *statement* of lem:bu and in the definition of
$U(S,t)$ (eq. (U)) BEFORE the "symmetrized convention"
$\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(AN):=\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(N^{1/2}AN^{1/2})$ is introduced (that definition appears later,
inside the $\Pi_S^\perp$/$\Pi_S$ computation). Since $M^{-2}B$ and $M^{-1}B$ are products of a
symmetric and a PSD matrix and are NOT symmetric in general, these symbols are undefined at
their point of first use unless the symmetrized convention is invoked there.

Moreover, for lem:bu's hypothesis to be the SAME quantity that is bounded by the SMW
computation, one needs the identity
\[
  \operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^{p}B) \;=\; \operatorname{Tr}_\sigma\!\big(B^{1/2}M^{p}B^{1/2}\big)
                       \;=\; \operatorname{Tr}_\sigma\!\big(C^\top M^{p}C\big),
  \qquad B = CC^\top,
\]
which holds because $B^{1/2}M^pB^{1/2}$ and $C^\top M^pC$ share the same nonzero spectrum
(the shared-nonzero-spectrum identity for $XY$ vs. $YX$). This identity is what makes the
hypothesis of lem:bu match the $U(S,t)$ used in Step 4 and the SMW term in the proof of
lem:bu; it is used implicitly but never stated.

**Severity.** Expository / faithfulness, not a logical error â€” I verified numerically that
all the identities hold. But as written, a reader cannot confirm that the symbol in the
lemma statement, the symbol in eq. (U), and the symbol produced by the SMW expansion denote
the same number without the missing convention being applied at first use and the
shared-spectrum identity being stated.

**Fix.** At the first appearance of $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^pB)$ (lemma statement), add: "all
mixed products $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(AN)$ with $A$ symmetric, $N\succeq0$ are read in the
symmetrized sense $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(N^{1/2}AN^{1/2})$, and $\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(M^pB)=
\operatorname{Tr}_\sigma(C^\top M^pC)$ for $B=CC^\top$ since the two matrices share their nonzero spectra."
